其實佢只係無端端改咗做暴力行為唔應該繼續落去
得個句扭曲咗

https://www.francemediasmonde.com/tw/contact
我驚如果用中文寄信都只係畀支那人擋返住，用有限公司英文投訴咗：

Hi, I am writing to express my deepest concern regarding one of your problematic journalists "Yang Mei" or "楊眉", her outrageous distortion of interview contents and blatant attempt to work as China's mouthpiece.

In Yang Mei’s problematic article, she purposely distorted the interviewees’ responses, dubbed it with subtitles that carry an opposite meaning and blatantly put words in the respondent’s mouth.

For example, the female respondent at 1:02 replies that:
“Hong Kong is a free country. It’s inappropriate to introduce China’s Security Law. Now we are an unfree and undemocratic country.”
Yang distorted the meaning and dubbed it as:
“Hong Kong is a free ‘region’ and violent activities should not go on. This isn’t freedom nor democracy.”
I found the professionalism and morality of journalist Yang highly dubious. She appears to me more like an endorser of China’s authoritarian regime who’s willing to create fake news any day of the week, instead of a neutral and fair journalist like she’s supposed to be. I’d like to express my strongest protest against this and will make sure to share this unfortunate news regarding RFI with all my friends.

On a side note, Traditional Chinese users do not use “” at all but use 「」instead. I’d like to ask RFI to look into this issue regarding Yang’s lack of morality and ability.

Thank you.

痴線中文直頭兩個意思

[文長慎入]中共已經｢哂冷｣，香港人選擇下注還是下跪？
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區
https://lih.kg/2029861

Template 都寫埋俾你，你唔係懶到copy & paste都唔肯呀？

In the news article “ 香港民眾如何看國安法香港版”, the reporter distorted the interviewee’s response. While the interviewee what the interviewee said is “香港是一個自由的國家 (I think Hong Kong is a free country”, the statement was distorted as “我認為香港是一個自由的地區 (I think Hong Kong is a free REGION”. Moreover, the interviewee didn’t criticise or blame on the actions taken by the protesters, but the reporter added that “暴力活動不應該持續下去 (Violence should not go on)”,which is her own opinion. Such blatant distortion should not be tolerated. I hereby request RFI to clarify and apologise for such grave mistakes.

In the news article “ 香港民眾如何看國安法香港版”, the reporter distorted the interviewee’s response. While the interviewee what the interviewee said is “香港是一個自由的國家 (I think Hong Kong is a free country”, the statement was distorted as “我認為香港是一個自由的地區 (I think Hong Kong is a free REGION”. Moreover, the interviewee didn’t criticise or blame on the actions taken by the protesters, but the reporter added that “暴力活動不應該持續下去 (Violence should not go on)”,which is her own opinion. Such blatant distortion should not be tolerated. I hereby request RFI to clarify and apologise for such grave mistakes.

我驚如果用中文寄信都只係畀支那人擋返住，用有限公司英文投訴咗：

Hi, I am writing to express my deepest concern regarding one of your problematic journalists "Yang Mei" or "楊眉", her outrageous distortion of interview contents and blatant attempt to work as China's mouthpiece.

In Yang Mei’s problematic article, she purposely distorted the interviewees’ responses, dubbed it with subtitles that carry an opposite meaning and blatantly put words in the respondent’s mouth.

For example, the female respondent at 1:02 replies that:
“Hong Kong is a free country. It’s inappropriate to introduce China’s Security Law. Now we are an unfree and undemocratic country.”
Yang distorted the meaning and dubbed it as:
“Hong Kong is a free ‘region’ and violent activities should not go on. This isn’t freedom nor democracy.”
I found the professionalism and morality of journalist Yang highly dubious. She appears to me more like an endorser of China’s authoritarian regime who’s willing to create fake news any day of the week, instead of a neutral and fair journalist like she’s supposed to be. I’d like to express my strongest protest against this and will make sure to share this unfortunate news regarding RFI with all my friends.

On a side note, Traditional Chinese users do not use “” at all but use 「」instead. I’d like to ask RFI to look into this issue regarding Yang’s lack of morality and ability.

Thank you.

Rfi d新聞好奇怪

https://lihkg.com/thread/2030973/page/1?post=1

Dear editor,

I am writing to report that an editor has twisted what an interviewee said in one of your new articles.
The article in question is
香港民眾如何看國安法香港版
作者：楊眉|視頻：楊眉
http://www.rfi.fr/tw/%E6%B8%AF%E6%BE%B3%E5%8F%B0/20200522-%E9%A6%99%E6%B8%AF%E6%B0%91%E7%9C%BE%E5%A6%82%E4%BD%95%E7%9C%8B%E5%9C%8B%E5%AE%89%E6%B3%95%E9%A6%99%E6%B8%AF%E7%89%88

In the video, the second interviewee said that the national security law is not one bit suitable for Hong Kong. Instead, the article reads "the violence should not continue". Such is a blatant twist of what the interviewee said to fit the editor's own agenda. Such distortion not only misleads readers but also harms the reputation as a credible news organization. Please correct the mistakes and revise other works done by the editor.

Reader

敬啟者：

你好，在閱讀你們的新聞時發現以下新聞中，受訪者的言論被惡意扭曲。
香港民眾如何看國安法香港版
作者：楊眉|視頻：楊眉
http://www.rfi.fr/tw/%E6%B8%AF%E6%BE%B3%E5%8F%B0/20200522-%E9%A6%99%E6%B8%AF%E6%B0%91%E7%9C%BE%E5%A6%82%E4%BD%95%E7%9C%8B%E5%9C%8B%E5%AE%89%E6%B3%95%E9%A6%99%E6%B8%AF%E7%89%88

文中的第二名受訪者說：「他（中國）無端加插（國安法）是一點都不適合」，但是新聞居然寫成了「暴力活動不應該持續下去」，這簡直是作者把自己的話塞進了受訪者的口中。這樣惡意扭曲受訪者的言論小則是誤導讀者，大則影響你們的新聞聲譽，請盡快改正和檢視這位作者其他新聞的水準。

讀者

https://www.youtube.com/user/RFIcn
入佢YT睇講香港既片, 入面comment 9成9 5毛
